title: Security-critical function docs expose placeholder behavior as normative
description:
Some generated function pages include placeholder algorithm text in the main definition block without a strong failure-style warning.

Impact:
- Readers can misinterpret placeholder logic as real protocol behavior.
- Security and compliance reviews may consume inaccurate semantics.
- The docs currently blur the line between proved contract and placeholder implementation details.

Observed pattern:
- Function pages show placeholder code/comments in the same visual hierarchy as real definitions.
- Existing wording is not forceful enough to prevent misuse.

Expected:
- Placeholder/stub/uninterpreted behavior must be visually and semantically explicit.
- Generated page should lead with an unmissable warning banner and machine-readable status metadata.
- Placeholder definitions should not be presented as primary algorithm text.

Acceptance criteria:
1) Add explicit status field in each function page (e.g., Verified/Assumed/Stub/Placeholder).
2) For Placeholder/Stub, render warning block before signature and definition.
3) Optionally suppress full placeholder body unless a "show modeling details" section is expanded.
4) Add snapshot tests to lock warning wording and placement.

title: Generated docs have massive broken relative-link surface (396 dead links)
description:
The generated markdown is not navigable at scale: 396 relative links resolve to missing files.

Impact:
- Readers cannot jump from signatures to type/function/property docs.
- Property cross-references and call graph drill-downs are effectively broken.
- This undermines trust in generated docs and blocks review workflows.

Evidence from current workspace generation:
- Broken links total: 396
- Affected output sets: out_SDEP (215), out_chacha20 (45), out_Blake2b (34), out_trivium (32), out_ZUC (32), out_SHA2 (17), out_SHA2_new (17), out_internal_single (4)
- Example failing refs include links with extra namespace/path prefixes that do not exist in generated output location.

Expected:
- Every emitted relative markdown link resolves to an existing local target.
- Link generation should be based on output-root-relative paths, not source namespace paths.

Acceptance criteria:
1) Add a generation-time link integrity check that fails build when any local markdown link is dead.
2) Regenerate docs for all example modules with zero dead links.
3) Add regression test over fixtures that validates local link existence.

title: Module parameters render as empty names/raw kinds and unhelpful bullets
description:
Generator bug: module parameter section can render malformed entries like '**`** : #' and present constraints as raw bullet text. This looks like a parsing + presentation failure.

Repro:
- examples/out_SHA2/index.md shows:
  - **`** : #
  - *Constraint:* (w % 32 == 0, 32 <= w, 64 >= w)
  - **`** : #
  - *Constraint:* (8 * w >= DigestSize, DigestSize % 8 == 0)

Expected:
- Parameter names and kinds are parsed and rendered correctly (no empty identifier placeholders).
- Constraints appear under an accordion/details block and include a plain-English explanation in addition to formal text.
- Parameters/constraints should use structured layout (table or field grid), not loose bullets.
- Add a short blurb explaining what module parameters are and why they matter for the module.

Acceptance criteria:
1) SHA2 module parameter names no longer render empty.
2) Constraint block includes English paraphrase + formal constraint in collapsible section.
3) Snapshot test covers malformed-empty-name regression and layout format.

title: Renderer fix only applied to some outputs; generated corpora now have split formats
description: Scoped re-review excluding demo_output still finds widespread broken fragment links in generated specs. Root cause: type aliases are emitted as bold paragraphs instead of heading anchors in several corpora, while function docs still link to fragments like ../types.md#round, #block, #validmessagelength, etc. Confirmed affected non-demo outputs: examples/out_chacha20, examples/out_SHA2, examples/out_trivium, examples/out_ZUC, test_output_chacha. Evidence: examples/out_chacha20/types.md line 3 defines Round as bold text, while examples/out_chacha20/functions/core.md lines 6 and 9 link to #round/#block. examples/out_SHA2/types.md lines 5, 13, 21 are bold alias declarations while examples/out_SHA2/functions/pad.md lines 9, 15, 23 link to #validmessagelength/#numblocks/#blocksize. test_output_chacha shows same pattern (types.md line 3 vs functions/core.md line 3). Expected: enforce one anchorable type-rendering format across all generated outputs, and add post-generation validation that fails on unresolved markdown fragments.

title: Call graph omits functions listed in generated function table
description:
Generator bug: the generated function inventory table includes functions that are missing from the rendered call graph in the same output.

Repro (current workspace):
- examples/out_SDEP/functions/index.md lists 16 functions in the table
- same file's Mermaid call graph renders only 7 function nodes
- examples/out_SDEP/index.md shows the same incomplete graph

Impact:
- Docs become internally inconsistent and misleading about available functions and connectivity.
- Users cannot distinguish between truly isolated functions and functions accidentally omitted by graph generation.

Expected behavior:
- Every function in the generated function table appears as a node in the call graph, even if it has no edges (isolated node).
- Edge set remains accurate for known calls; node completeness is guaranteed.

Likely area:
- Call graph assembly stage before Markdown rendering (node set derived only from edge endpoints).
- Mermaid renderer should emit full declared function node set + edges.

Acceptance criteria:
1) Add regression test fixture/snapshot that fails when table function count exceeds graph node count for same module output.
2) Ensure SDEP output includes nodes for functions like provisionKey/enrollDevice/authenticate/enforceAccess/getStatus/verifierTimestamp_current even when they have zero outgoing/incoming edges.
3) Fix implemented in generator pipeline (not manual docs patching).

title: Function descriptions may appear truncated in generated markdown
description:
Generator bug: some function descriptions look cut off in output markdown, even when source comments are multi-line and complete.

Repro context:
- Source comment in tests/fixtures/SDEP.cry includes a multi-line paragraph beginning with:
  'The timestamp the verifier validates must equal the timestamp inside the'
- Users report rendered function docs showing this as if truncated/cut off.

Expected:
- Multi-line contiguous comment blocks are preserved as complete prose in generated function descriptions.
- Line wrapping and paragraph joins are stable and never drop continuation lines.
- Snapshot coverage catches truncation regressions for long wrapped comment blocks.

Likely area:
- Comment capture/normalization in parser -> IR description fields
- Markdown rendering of wrapped paragraphs in function pages

Acceptance criteria:
1) Add/adjust fixture + snapshot test that fails if continuation lines are dropped.
2) Ensure generated file for verifierTimestamp_current and similar long descriptions renders full paragraph text.
3) Confirm no docs-only patching; fix must be in generator pipeline.

title: Generated docs overstate bounded proofs as general guarantees
description: The generated text currently presents bounded-model results with language that reads like global correctness claims. In examples/out_SDEP/types.md line 128, the narrative says P23 proves injectivity and then states the argument extends to arbitrary n in the same rendered spec output. This blurs the boundary between machine-checked bounded properties and informal extrapolation, which is dangerous in security-facing specs. Expected: renderer should clearly label bounded claims as bounded, isolate informal rationale as non-proof notes (or omit it), and avoid wording that can be read as universally proved behavior.

title: Type cross-references are broken across generated docs due missing heading anchors
description: Generated function/property pages link to anchors like types.md#block, #state, #requests, #fls, etc., but the target type aliases are emitted as bold paragraphs (e.g. **Block** — 16) instead of headings. Example: examples/out_Blake2b/functions/F.md line 3 links to ../types.md#block, but examples/out_Blake2b/types.md defines Block on line 3 as bold text, not a heading, so the fragment anchor is invalid. Same failure pattern appears throughout out_SDEP/demo_output as well (e.g. canonicalizeS signature links to #requests/#fls/#ns). Expected: emit stable heading anchors for all referenced type symbols or generate links to valid anchors.

title: Generated SDEP properties link to non-existent helper function pages (none/some)
description: The generated file examples/out_SDEP/properties/key-lifecycle-safety.md contains dead links to ../functions/none.md and ../functions/some.md (lines 48 and 62). Those files are not generated under examples/out_SDEP/functions/, so navigation and traceability are broken for P3/P4. Repro: open key-lifecycle-safety.md and click the links; target files do not exist. Expected: either emit none.md/some.md in this corpus or stop linking to them and reference the actual Option/constructor representation directly.

title: Accordion code block leaks next function's doc comment and body
description: In function pages, the 'Formal definition (Cryptol)' accordion includes source from the NEXT function. E.g. hmac.md's accordion contains hmac_truncate's full doc comment and signature after hmac's body. The renderer is not properly delimiting where one function's source ends and the next begins.

title: Function doc comments are not rendered in function pages
description: Rich doc comments on functions are discarded. E.g. hmac has a detailed block comment with description ('Compute a pseudorandom block using the HMAC construction as specified in [FIPS-198-1], Section 4'), type parameter docs, parameter docs (K, text), and spec references. None of this appears in hmac.md — only an auto-generated one-liner ('Computes a [L][8] from K and text'). The full doc comment content should be rendered as the function description.

title: Type doc comments are not rendered on the types page
description: Doc comments on type aliases are not included in types.md. E.g. HMAC.cry has '/** An upper bound on the HMAC key size (in bytes). */ type KeySizeUpperBound = B' but types.md only shows 'KeySizeUpperBound - B' with no description. The doc comment text should be rendered alongside each type definition.

title: Module parameters are missing from rendered output
description: Cryptol module parameters (the 'parameter' block) are not rendered anywhere in the generated docs. E.g. HMAC.cry declares 'type B : #' (block size), 'type L : #' (output size), type constraints, and 'H' (the hash function) — none of these appear in the output. These are essential for understanding the spec. They should be rendered prominently (e.g. on index.md or types.md) with their doc comments, constraints, and signatures.

title: Render module-level doc comment as formatted content in index.md
description: The opening block comment on a Cryptol module contains rich semantic info: title, description, copyright, authors, references with DOIs/URLs, security notes, etc. (e.g. HMAC.cry has FIPS citations, author info, security warnings). This should be parsed and rendered as well-formatted markdown in the index.md header — not discarded. Include: module description, authors, references as proper links, and any security/usage notes.

title: Empty category slug produces .md filename
description: Properties without a category section get an empty slug, producing '.md' as filename instead of 'misc.md'. Fixed with fallback in linker.rs

title: Private blocks skipped by parser
description: Grammar skips private keyword blocks entirely, so declarations inside (properties and functions) are not rendered. Fixed by returning private block spans as SigOrBind and splitting declarations in parser.rs

title: Mermaid: protocol function call graph on index page
description: Add a mermaid directed graph to index.md showing which functions reference other functions. Scan function body text for cross-references (extend existing linker word-boundary scan). Only emit when cross-function references exist. Use mermaid-builder crate.

title: Render manifest not_attempted/failed entries even when property absent from spec
description: If a proof_manifest.json entry has status not_attempted or failed but no matching property exists in the parsed Cryptol spec (e.g. the property was removed or the name/label lookup misses), the renderer currently silently drops it. These gaps are exactly the most important things to surface. Fix: render a placeholder section for every manifest entry whose status is not_attempted or failed, regardless of whether a corresponding property node was found. Use the manifest key (name or label) as the heading and emit the status badge + any message/counterexample present.

title: Adapter: raw SAW prove_print logs -> proof_manifest.json
description: The existing --adapt-saw-results flag only ingests saw-spec-gen result.json. Add a second adapter that parses raw SAW prove_print / prove output logs (lines like 'Q.E.D.', 'Counterexample', property name, etc.) and emits a proof_manifest.json in the same schema. This lets projects that run SAW directly (without saw-spec-gen) feed verification results into the renderer without a custom glue script.

title: Property-label collisions in manifest silently resolve wrong entry
description: p5_disabled_rejects_create and p5_disabled_rejects_attest both render with label p5. The resolver tries module::label, then label, then module::name, then name — so when two properties share a label, the collision is silent and the wrong entry is picked. Workaround: use property names (not labels) as manifest keys to disambiguate. Fix should either (a) detect duplicate labels at manifest-parse time and error, or (b) demote label-only lookup so name always wins, or (c) warn when a label-keyed lookup is ambiguous.

title: Add --docfx flag to generate toc.yml files for DocFX compatibility
description: Add a CLI flag (e.g. --docfx or --format docfx) that switches the renderer to produce DocFX-compatible output. Key requirements: generate toc.yml alongside each index.md summarizing the module/section hierarchy; ensure index.md front-matter (uid, title) conforms to DocFX YAML header conventions; toc.yml entries should link to functions/, properties/, and types/ sections. Should be opt-in so default Markdown output is unchanged.

title: main.rs: hardcoded markdown strings in Rust
description: Markdown hardcoded in src/main.rs. render_multi_module_index: L417 H1 heading, L419 H2 heading, L420-421 table header+separator, L426 table row with md links, L432 H2 heading, L435-439 list items with links, L445 H2 heading, L446-447 mermaid code fence. render_type_check_report: L876 H1 heading, L877-878 table header+separator, L894 table row with inline code (TypeAlias/EnumGroup), L934 table row with inline code (RecordType).

title: main.rs: hardcoded markdown strings in Rust
description:
Markdown is hardcoded directly in Rust string literals in src/main.rs. Specific instances:

render_multi_module_index (~L417-450):
  L417: H1 heading "# Specification Modules"
  L419: H2 heading "## Modules"
  L420-421: table header row + separator row
  L426: table data row with markdown links to module pages
  L432: H2 heading "## Module Hierarchy"
  L435-439: list items with links (indented per depth)
  L445: H2 heading "## Dependencies"
  L446-447: mermaid code fence open/body

render_type_check_report (~L876-934):
  L876: H1 heading "# Type Cross-Check"
  L877-878: table header row + separator row
  L894: table data row with inline code spans (TypeAlias/EnumGroup)
  L934: table data row with inline code spans (RecordType)

title: render_md.rs: hardcoded markdown strings in Rust
description:
Markdown is hardcoded directly in Rust string literals in src/render_md.rs. Specific instances:

Headings:
- L108: writeln!(out, \

title: Property index tables contain duplicate IDs and ambiguous numbering
description:
At least one generated property index contains duplicate property IDs (same ID appears multiple times with different labels/descriptions).

Impact:
- Breaks traceability from prose/spec documents to generated property index.
- Confuses downstream review and verification reporting.
- Makes anchors and references ambiguous for automation.

Observed in current generated output:
- Duplicate IDs detected in a property summary table (e.g., repeated P5 and repeated P19 entries in one index).

Expected:
- Each property ID appears exactly once per module-level index.
- If multiple scenarios exist for one property family, represent them as sub-bullets under a single canonical property row.

Acceptance criteria:
1) Add validation pass that fails generation on duplicate property IDs in an index page.
2) Normalize table generation to canonical one-row-per-property-ID form.
3) Add regression test fixture with repeated scenarios ensuring stable, unambiguous rendering.

title: Generator emits degenerate near-empty pages instead of pruning
description:
The generator still emits many pages containing only a heading plus "No declarations were parsed" style text.

Impact:
- Bloats output tree with low-value pages.
- Creates misleading perception of missing content versus intentionally absent sections.
- Increases navigation noise and weakens generated-doc quality.

Measured in current output:
- 14 markdown files with <=2 non-empty lines.
- Includes module-level and subsection index pages, not only top-level indexes.

Expected:
- Do not emit leaf pages for empty sections.
- Parent indexes should omit links to absent sections and show concise "not applicable" summaries where needed.

Acceptance criteria:
1) Suppress generation of empty types/functions/properties pages across all nesting levels.
2) Ensure parent index tables hide links to suppressed pages.
3) Snapshot tests assert no emitted markdown file has <=2 non-empty lines unless explicitly allowed.

title: Hide empty top-level sections for modules without types/functions/properties
description: In module index markdown, omit Types/Functions/Security Properties sections entirely when there are no parsed entries, instead of rendering empty-section placeholders.

title: Show stub/uninterpreted modeling badges and inline warnings
description: When a function is modeled as a stub/uninterpreted contract, show a visible badge and an inline warning near the function summary/definition. Ensure this appears both in function listings and function detail pages.

title: Add verification badge tooltips and explicit warning treatment for Assumed
description: Implement hoverable badge tooltips for Verified and Assumed statuses in markdown output. Assumed must render as a warning-style badge with clear trust-contract wording. Apply consistently in index, function pages, and property pages.

title: Remove top-level Reading Guide and shift to inline contextual hints
description: Replace the module-level Reading Guide block with inline guidance located where users need it (functions list, properties list, formal definition blocks). Keep hints concise and contextual; avoid abstract upfront prose.

title: Add SAW newcomer helper text to generated docs
description:
Documentation clarity gap: generated specs assume SAW/Cryptol familiarity. Readers new to SAW need contextual helper text to interpret what they are seeing.

Goal:
- Improve comprehension for non-SAW readers without diluting formal accuracy.

Expected behavior:
- Generated docs include concise helper text that explains core concepts in plain language, such as:
  - What SAW verification means in this context
  - What a property is vs a function definition
  - Meaning of type parameters and constraints
  - How to read 'Formal definition (Cryptol)' blocks
  - What stubs/uninterpreted functions imply in proofs
- Helper text should be reusable boilerplate emitted by generator (not hand-edited per file).

Suggested placement:
- Short 'How to read this spec' section near top-level index.md
- Contextual one-liners near signature/property/formal-proof sections

Acceptance criteria:
1) At least one top-level explainer section is generated for each module output.
2) Signature/property sections include concise newcomer-friendly helper text where needed.
3) Snapshot tests verify helper text presence and wording stability.
4) Implementation is in generation pipeline (renderer/templates), not manual output edits.

title: Signature sections should use structured table + explain type parameters
description:
Generator UX bug: function signatures currently render as bullet lists (Type Parameters, Constraints, Parameters, Returns) without clear explanation of what type parameters mean.

Repro:
- examples/out_SHA2/functions/ROTL.md and similar pages show bullet-style signature blocks.

Expected:
- Render signature details in a structured table layout rather than loose bullets.
- Include a short explanatory blurb/tool-tip text for type parameters (e.g., compile-time polymorphic parameters such as widths/lengths, not runtime arguments).
- Keep formal raw signature section, but improve readability of the primary signature presentation.

Acceptance criteria:
1) Signature blocks render as table format for type parameters, constraints, parameters, and return type.
2) Generated pages include concise explanation of 'Type Parameters'.
3) Snapshot tests updated for at least one polymorphic function signature (e.g., ROTL).

title: Private definitions are not labeled in rendered function docs
description:
Generator bug: functions/types declared in a private block are rendered like public items with no visibility indicator.

Repro:
- examples/SHA2.cry declares private block containing ROTL/ROTR/SHR/sigma0/sigma1/etc.
- Generated docs (e.g., examples/out_SHA2/functions/index.md and corresponding function pages) do not mark these entries as private.

Expected:
- Private items are clearly marked as private in rendered output.
- Visibility should be visible both in function index table and individual function page header/signature area.
- Optional: style hint in call graph legend if private nodes are included there.

Acceptance criteria:
1) Visibility metadata from parser/IR is preserved to renderer.
2) Private items display a consistent 'Private' badge/column/label in generated markdown.
3) Snapshot test verifies a known private SHA2 function renders with private marker.

title: Auto-generated property summaries are too vague for literal correctness checks
description:
Generator content-quality bug: summaries like 'Hex digits encode correctly: asserts the expected outcome.' are too generic and fail to state the concrete expected relation.

Repro:
- examples/out_SHA2/properties/misc.md has vague summaries for hex/word/integer encoding properties.

Expected:
- For simple literal/equality properties, synthesize specific summaries (e.g., mention exact equivalence being asserted, such as hex literal equals corresponding bit pattern).
- Keep concise but concrete, not template boilerplate.

Acceptance criteria:
1) Summary generation includes property-specific details when expression shape allows.
2) Existing generic fallback remains only when structure cannot be summarized safely.
3) Snapshot tests for hexDigitsEncodeCorrectly/wordsEncodeCorrectly/integersEncodeCorrectly become more specific.

title: Improve call-graph palette contrast for dark backgrounds
description:
Generator styling bug: current graph palette can be hard to read in dark-theme Mermaid renderers (node fills/labels/strokes blend or appear low-contrast).

Expected:
- Adopt a higher-contrast, theme-robust palette for default/stub/decision nodes.
- Validate readability in dark and light renderers.

Acceptance criteria:
1) classDef colors updated for better contrast and legibility.
2) Snapshot test pins updated classDef lines.
3) No manual doc tweaks; renderer-level change only.

title: Fallback security-property layout when no meaningful categories exist
description:
Generator UX bug: when security properties do not naturally group into meaningful categories, index should show a flat property table instead of forcing category presentation.

Expected behavior:
- If category metadata is absent/trivial/single catch-all bucket, render property rows directly in a table on index.md.
- Preserve links and ordering deterministically.

Acceptance criteria:
1) Add fallback rendering path for uncategorized or only-generic-category cases.
2) Snapshot test demonstrates flat table output on such input.

title: Type links should omit .md suffix in rendered markdown references
description:
Generator consistency issue: references like '[types.md](types.md)' should avoid exposing file extension in link text where style prefers logical doc names.

Repro:
- examples/out_SHA2/index.md contains 'All type definitions: [types.md](types.md)'

Expected:
- Link text should be 'types' (or 'Types') while href may remain types.md.
- Apply same convention consistently to similar index references.

Acceptance criteria:
1) Generated markdown no longer displays '.md' in user-facing link text for standard section links.
2) Snapshot coverage added for index link text formatting.

title: Adopt standard, accessible color theme for generated function graphs
description:
Generator bug/UI quality issue: generated function graphs use hard-coded colors that are visually poor and non-standard for Mermaid diagrams, reducing readability.

Feedback summary:
- Function graph colors are not acceptable and should follow a more standard style.

Repro context:
- Current emitted Mermaid classDefs use bright custom colors (blue/green/orange) with thin contrast handling.
- Color definitions are emitted from renderer logic (not authored docs), so this must be fixed in generator output.

Expected behavior:
- Use a conventional Mermaid-friendly palette with readable contrast for nodes/labels/edges.
- Keep semantic distinction between default/decision/stub nodes, but align with standard visual conventions and accessibility.
- Theme should render clearly in common light and dark markdown viewers where Mermaid is supported.

Likely area:
- Markdown renderer graph style emission for classDef lines.

Acceptance criteria:
1) Replace hard-coded graph class colors with a standardized palette and contrast-safe text colors.
2) Snapshot tests updated to lock the new classDef output and prevent regressions.
3) Change is generator-level (src renderer), not manual edits to generated docs.

title: Renderer leaks raw internal proof commentary into public type docs
description: The generated types pages are polluted with long internal editorial/proof-process text that is not type documentation. Example: examples/out_SDEP/types.md line 128 injects a full historical narrative (counterexample history, FIX notes, BMC scope, future theorem prover suggestions) into the FL type description. Line 136 similarly embeds inline source-comment text (2     // field length bound, bytes) into rendered docs. This makes generated specs noisy, unstable, and hard to consume, and mixes process notes with normative spec output. Expected: sanitize/comment-filter descriptions and keep rendered type docs concise and structured.

title: Render function signature as structured parameters/return type, not raw string
description: Function pages currently show the raw Cryptol signature as a single string (e.g. '{k, n} (width (8 * k) <= B, ...) => [k][8] -> [n][8] -> [L][8]'). Instead, break it down into structured, nicely formatted sections: type parameters, constraints, parameters (with names and types), and return type. Each type reference should link to its definition. This makes signatures readable without Cryptol expertise.

title: Generate English-language descriptions of properties
description: Property pages currently show only the raw Cryptol definition. The renderer should also generate a plain-English translation/summary of what each property asserts, making them accessible to readers who don't know Cryptol. E.g. a property like 'hmac_deterministic K msg = hmac K msg == hmac K msg' should also say something like 'HMAC is deterministic: computing the MAC of the same key and message twice always produces the same result.'

title: Types page should cross-link to functions that use each type
description: On types.md, each type definition should include back-links to the functions that reference it. E.g. 'KeySizeUpperBound = B' should link to hmac, expand_key, etc. that use B. This makes the type page navigable and useful for understanding where types flow.

title: Block comment delimiter leaks into rendered descriptions
description: The /** block comment opening delimiter is appearing in rendered function descriptions. E.g. on HMAC index.md, ipad shows: '/** Inner pad; the byte x''36'' repeated B times.' — the leading /** should be stripped during comment extraction.

title: Cryptol type syntax in descriptions is opaque to readers
description: Raw Cryptol type notation like [L][8] appears in function descriptions (e.g. hmac: 'Computes a [L][8] from K and text'). Non-Cryptol readers have no idea this means 'a sequence of L bytes'. The renderer should either: (1) translate common Cryptol type patterns into plain English (e.g. [N][8] -> 'N bytes'), (2) link type parameters to their definitions, or (3) at minimum add a tooltip/footnote explaining the notation.

title: Security properties should link to involved types and functions
description: On function pages, each security property listed should include hyperlinks to the types and functions that are involved in / referenced by that property, making it easy to navigate the spec.

title: Function definition leaks outside accordion on function pages
description: On function pages (e.g. authenticate.md), the raw Cryptol definition (e.g. 'authenticate dateValid signatureValid claimsValid = dateValid && signatureValid && claimsValid') appears outside the accordion/details section as bare text. This definition is already shown inside the accordion and should not be duplicated above it.

title: Remove Property Coverage mermaid diagram from index.md
description: The Property Coverage mermaid diagram on index.md is confusing and not helpful. Remove it from the rendered output entirely.

title: Mermaid diagram nodes should have clickable links to targets
description: In the call graph and other mermaid diagrams on index.md, each node (function/type) should be a clickable link that navigates to the corresponding generated markdown page (e.g. functions/authenticate.md).

title: Module-aware output structure for multi-file specs
description: When rendering a multi-module spec, the output should organize by module — e.g., a top-level index listing all modules, per-module subdirectories or sections, and a dependency graph showing which modules import which. The index page should show the module hierarchy and link to each module's types/functions/properties.

title: Cross-module symbol resolution in linker
description: The linker (SymbolTable) only resolves symbols defined within a single module. For multi-file specs, it needs to: (1) build a unified symbol table across all modules, (2) resolve references to imported symbols to the correct output file, (3) generate cross-module markdown links (e.g., a property in module A referencing a function from module B should link to ../moduleB/functions/fn.md).

title: Support multi-file input (directory or file list)
description: The CLI currently takes a single .cry file. Need to accept either a directory of .cry files or multiple file arguments. The tool should discover all .cry files, determine module names (from 'module ... where' declarations or filenames), and feed them into the pipeline as a cohesive spec.

title: Add IR representation for imports and cross-module references
description: The IR (ir.rs) has no concept of imports or cross-module symbols. Need an Item::Import variant (or similar) that carries the module path, and a mechanism in the IR to distinguish locally-defined symbols from imported ones. This is prerequisite for cross-module linking.

title: Parse import declarations instead of discarding them
description: The grammar currently matches 'import' lines and discards them (returns None). The AST has a data-less Import variant. Need to: (1) capture the imported module path, optional qualifier (as), and optional hiding list in the grammar, (2) populate the AST Import variant with that data, (3) propagate through the parser to downstream stages.

title: Mermaid: decision flowcharts for multi-branch functions
description: Add mermaid flowchart diagrams to function pages that have >2 branches. Diamond nodes for conditions, rounded boxes for results, yes/no edges. Derives from existing Branch IR data. Skip for single-branch functions and enum constants. Use mermaid-builder crate.

title: saw-results-to-manifest adapter script
description: Write a PowerShell or Rust script that reads saw-spec-gen result.json (per-function SAT/UNSAT verdicts) and emits a proof_manifest.json in pretty-specs format. Optionally reads pretty-specs JSON IR to infer property-level status from function-level results via the related_properties mapping.

title: Extend proof manifest to accept function names as keys
description: load_proof_manifest already returns HashMap<String, ProofStatus>. Extend the main.rs apply-manifest loop to match function names (not just property labels) and inject proof_status into Item::Function. No schema change needed - the flat format already works since function names and property labels are disjoint.

title: Add proof_status field to Item::Function
description: Add proof_status: Option<ProofStatus> to Item::Function in ir.rs, and extend the markdown renderer to show proof badges on function pages (same as properties). This lets the proof manifest attach verification status to functions, not just properties.

title: Auto-generate plain-text property descriptions from Cryptol expressions
description: Property pages just show whatever the Cryptol comments say, which may be minimal. The tool should auto-generate human-readable descriptions from the Cryptol expression. E.g. for 'enforceAccess AM_Enforce AD_Deny == (False, True)' generate 'When access mode is Enforce and decision is Deny, result is (allowed=False, logged=True)'. Approach: pattern-match on ==> (preconditions), == (expected outcome), != (exclusion), function calls with enum literals. Translate ==> as 'Given that...', == as 'the result is...'. Even a mechanical translation is more accessible than raw Cryptol.

title: Function signatures render poorly for bare definitions
description: Functions with explicit type signatures render correctly, but bare definitions (e.g. 'provisionKey fleetEnabled validRequest vaultResult keyIsActive = ...') render parameter names as the signature instead of types. 'none' has a completely empty signature. Root cause: parse_bare_function extracts tokens between name and = as sig_parts, which are param names not types. When a function has both a type sig line and a definition line, the parser should link them. For bare defs, show params->result format or indicate it is a bare definition.

title: Enum fallback description 'A {width} value' is confusing
description: When an enum type has no doc comment, the renderer falls back to 'A {width} value.' which produces confusing text like 'A 2 value.' or 'A 3 value.' (the width is the bit-vector width). Should instead say something like 'A {width}-bit enumeration with N variants.' or list the variants, or simply omit the description. See render_md.rs EnumGroup branch when doc.is_empty().

title: Types not listed in index.md - only a link to types.md
description: The index page renders a full table for functions and properties but for types just emits 'All type definitions: types.md'. Should include a summary table of types with name, kind (enum/record/alias), and description, similar to the functions table. See render_index() in render_md.rs where the types section is hardcoded to a single link.

title: Section numbers leak into function descriptions
description: Function descriptions in the index include section numbering from Cryptol source comments (e.g. enforceAccess shows '4.5 enforceAccess', enrollDevice shows '4.2 enrollDevice (activate)'). These are comment lines like '// 4.5 enforceAccess' picked up as function doc. The tool should strip leading section numbers and, if the remaining text is just the function name, treat the description as empty or auto-generate one.

title: Separator lines (////) leak into descriptions and type docs
description: Comment separator lines like ///.../// are leaking into rendered output. provisionKey's description in the index is literally the separator. FleetMode enum's doc starts with the separator. Root cause: strip_comment_prefix removes leading // but remaining /////... passes through as doc text. is_separator_line only fires for three-line section-title patterns; standalone separators fall through to pending_doc. Separator lines should never appear in rendered doc text.

title: some/none rendered as top-level functions with no description
description: some and none from the Cryptol spec are Option constructors (OptUUID helpers), not standalone functions. The tool renders them as the first two entries in the function table with empty descriptions. They should be recognized as constructors and rendered alongside the type definition, filtered out of the functions list, or have auto-generated descriptions. Root cause: parser treats bare name=expr definitions the same as decision-logic functions.

title: Line-wrapping artifacts leak into rendered prose (token fragmentation)
description:
Generated markdown includes line-wrapping artifacts that fragment terms mid-sentence (e.g., a technical term split with trailing hyphen at line end).

Impact:
- Reduces readability and perceived quality.
- Can distort searchability and copy/paste fidelity.
- Signals formatting instability in generator output.

Observed:
- At least one function page has a hard-wrapped fragment in descriptive prose.

Expected:
- Renderer should avoid splitting technical tokens across lines in output markdown.
- Keep sentence-level wrapping natural, or do not hard-wrap generated prose.

Acceptance criteria:
1) Adjust wrapping strategy to avoid mid-token hyphenation in generated text.
2) Add snapshot check for known problematic description blocks.
3) Ensure regenerated docs have zero token-fragment artifacts.

title: Trailing slash on 'functions/' link text in index.md
description: On index.md, the Functions section shows 'All function definitions: functions/' with a trailing slash in the link text. Should read 'functions' without the slash (it's a link, not a filesystem path).

title: Add hover tooltips to mermaid diagram nodes
description: Mermaid supports tooltip text on nodes. Where possible, add hover info to diagram nodes showing e.g. the function signature, brief description, or type summary. Investigate what mermaid tooltip support is available and use it.

title: Mermaid diagrams need more color/styling
description: The mermaid diagrams in index.md are insufficiently colorful. Add color styling to nodes/edges to make the diagrams more visually informative and easier to read.

title: Aggregate proof-status manifest across modules
description: The --proof-status flag currently takes a single JSON file mapping property names to PASS/FAIL. For multi-module specs, properties may be spread across modules and may share names (e.g. duplicated access-control properties). The manifest format and lookup logic need to handle module-qualified property names or a multi-module manifest structure.

title: Topological sort for module dependency order
description: When processing multiple files with inter-module imports, the tool needs to determine a valid processing order. Build a dependency graph from import declarations and topologically sort modules so that a module is processed after all its dependencies. Detect and report cycles.

title: Mermaid: property-function coverage map on index page
description: Add a mermaid bipartite graph to index.md connecting property labels to functions they mention. Uses existing SymbolTable.related_properties data (inverted). Makes coverage gaps visible as unconnected function nodes. Skip when no properties exist. Use mermaid-builder crate.

title: Extended proof manifest format with per-language function status
description: Support a richer manifest format with top-level 'properties' and 'functions' keys, where functions can have per-language (cpp/rust) proof status entries including impl_file. Backward-compatible: detect flat format (property labels as top-level keys) vs structured format automatically in load_proof_manifest.

title: Verification coverage summary in index.md
description: When proof statuses are available (via --proof-status), render a coverage summary table in index.md showing: Cryptol properties proven/total, C++ functions verified/total, Rust functions verified/total, cross-language equivalence status. Data is already present from the manifest; this is purely a rendering addition.

title: Type cross-check mode: Cryptol types vs implementation types
description: Add a --check-types mode that compares Cryptol enum widths and record layouts (from pretty-specs IR) against C++/Rust implementation types (from saw-spec-gen constraint output). Catches silent representation mismatches like Cryptol [2] enum vs C++ i8 discriminant. Static check, no SAW invocation needed.

title: Add --emit-function-list flag for saw-spec-gen consumption
description: Add a CLI flag that outputs a JSON array of { name, signature, arity } for every Item::Function in the parsed spec. This becomes the input for saw-spec-gen batch verification, eliminating the need to manually specify --cryptol-fn / --function pairs.
